req.title: Manager views team
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a team through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated manager can view a team via GET /api/teams, mandatory fields are validated, an audit record is written on success (HTTP 200/201 with entity id), and errors are returned as 400 (validation), 401 (unauthenticated), or 403 (forbidden role). I designed: (1) a positive EP test covering the happy path with a valid manager token and a known team id; (2) a negative NEG test covering the 401 unauthenticated path; (3) a negative DT test covering the 403 forbidden-role path; (4) a boundary BVA test probing the minimal valid team id (id=1, lowest boundary); (5) a state-transition STC test confirming an audit record exists after a successful view.

tests.name: test_manager_views_team_success
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A team with a known id exists
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token>
expected: Response body contains entity id matching the requested team id
Response body contains mandatory team fields
trace: R-WEB-012: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_manager_views_team_success(get_manager_token, existing_team_id):
    token = get_manager_token()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/teams/{existing_team_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["id"] == existing_team_id
    assert "name" in body

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401
actions: Send GET /api/teams/{team_id} without any Authorization header
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401(existing_team_id):
    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/teams/{existing_team_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 401

tests.name: test_non_manager_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but holds role 'employee' (not manager)
A team with a known id exists
actions: Obtain a valid employee auth token via <get_employee_token>
Send GET /api/teams/{team_id} with employee Authorization header
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_non_manager_role_returns_403(get_employee_token, existing_team_id):
    token = get_employee_token()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/teams/{existing_team_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403

tests.name: test_view_team_with_minimum_valid_id
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A team with id=1 exists (minimum boundary id)
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token>
trace: R-WEB-012: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-012: boundary – lowest valid team id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"
MIN_TEAM_ID = 1

def test_view_team_with_minimum_valid_id(get_manager_token):
    token = get_manager_token()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/teams/{MIN_TEAM_ID}", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["id"] == MIN_TEAM_ID

tests.name: test_audit_record_written_after_successful_view
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A team with a known id exists
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token>
Fetch the latest audit record via <get_latest_audit_record>
expected: Audit record exists referencing the team id and action VIEW
trace: R-WEB-012: state transition from pre-view to post-view with audit entry
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_audit_record_written_after_successful_view(
    get_manager_token, existing_team_id, get_latest_audit_record
):
    token = get_manager_token()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/teams/{existing_team_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity_id=existing_team_id, entity_type="team")
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit["action"] == "VIEW"
    assert audit["entity_id"] == existing_team_id
